text: Lung injuries caused by the tip of the trocov are quite common incidents that can occur in particular when the lung is still inflated or when pleural adhesions are present.
Repairing can be performed at the end of the surgery using absorbable treats and absorbable clips that can be applied to anchor and secure each end of the running suture.
The prophylactic fixation is usually performed to avoid residual parenchymal torsion around the bronchovascular pedicle after a major resection.
For this purpose suturing can be an easy and cost-saving option.
Stapling failure nowadays rarely occurs thanks to the tree stapling technology.
Should it however occur, a continuous suture can be performed to reinforce the stapling line and to ensure the hemostasis and the aerostasis.
Thoracoscopic suture can be used at the end of intraparenchymal lung resections.
In fact, even if different energetic devices allow coagulation and division of the lung tissue, hair leaks are frequent and additional sutures required.
In this clip intracorporeal knots are used for tying.
When parenchymal repairing is needed, barbered sutures provide a noteless alternative.
The bars penetrate the tissues and lock them into place, eliminating the need for nodes to tie the suture.
Several methods have been described to perform the bronchial anastomosis in thoracoscopic sliver resections.
In these two cases, a superior left and a superior right sleeve lobectomies are performed.
Interrupted suture are used for both the membranes and the cartilage membrane layers of the bronchus.
Extracorporeal knotting is used for tying.
